items.original_title: Oil transfer activity in Gulf of Oman slows following ship attacks, data shows
items.rss_summary: By Jonathan SaulLONDON, July 20 (Reuters) - Oil transfers between tankers in waters outside the Strait of Hormuz have slowed following a wave of recent attacks on vessels by Iranian forces, according to satellite analysis and sources.Since the start of the Iran war in late February, ship-to-ship (STS) transfers between tankers have helped enable oil to leave the Gulf despite the threats to commercial vessels sailing through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.rss_summary: Former Sen. Ben Sasse (R-Neb.) on Sunday defended the filibuster, saying the U.S. needs it in the Senate. Sasse wrote in a Wall Street Journal op-ed that the filibuster "provides a bulwark against consolidated power," a tradition preserved as "a prerequisite for national recovery and endurance." The filibuster is not a part of the Constitution,...

items.title: Атаки США усугубляют трудности, а не инакомыслие, на беспокойном юге Ирана
items.original_title: US attacks deepen hardship, not dissent, in Iran's restive south
items.rss_summary: By Parisa Hafezi and Angus McDowallDUBAI, July 20 (Reuters) - Opposition to Iran's clerical rulers has long simmered in the country's impoverished south. But after bearing the brunt of U.S. strikes this month, many residents are directing their anger at President Donald Trump and an air campaign they say has upended their lives.
